title: Heartroot Vault
alt: A vast ancient root system filling a cavern, a lone figure holding a golden light within

stages.label: Thumbnail
stages.text: Initial compositional study — exploring the tension between the vast root architecture and the lone figure carrying the only light source in the cavern.

stages.label: Final
stages.text: Finished spread. Warm amber tones from the lantern push against cool cave shadows. Root forms were designed to feel structural — like a living cathedral.

title: Whispers From The Mireglass
alt: A toxic swamp at night — gnarled dead trees surrounding a glowing green pool

stages.label: Thumbnail
stages.text: Compositional sketches testing the glowing pool as central focal point versus a framed mid-ground approach with silhouetted dead trees.

stages.label: Final
stages.text: Desaturated greens and sickly luminance from the pool. The dead tree silhouettes were designed to read as strong graphic shapes at 2" card scale.

title: Paradox Hollow
alt: A shattered portal arch in an arid desert wasteland under a pale sky

stages.label: Thumbnail
stages.text: Explored three angles: portal as horizon-breaker, portal as foreground frame, and worm's-eye low-angle. The horizon-breaker version best communicated scale.

stages.label: Final
stages.text: Bleached ochre palette with a pale, haze-washed sky. The shattered arch pieces were scattered deliberately — implying a catastrophic event rather than calm abandonment.

title: Mana Breach
alt: An arcane stone keep overrun with glowing magical tendrils and a mana wheel

stages.label: Thumbnail
stages.text: Early thumbnails focused on the mana wheel as the dominant shape, with the stone architecture playing a secondary, engulfed role.

stages.label: Final
stages.text: Cool stone greys contrasting with the warm amber-gold of the mana surge. The tendrils were rendered to feel both organic and mechanical — uncontrolled energy finding old paths.

title: The Ash Monolith
alt: A glowing blue crystal spire rising from a desert plain at sunset with mesas in the distance

stages.label: Thumbnail
stages.text: Testing the spire against different sky conditions — stormy, clear dusk, and haze. The sunset palette best justified the crystal's inner glow as a counterpoint light.

stages.label: Final
stages.text: Warm burnt sienna mesas against a deep violet-orange horizon. The cold blue of the crystal was kept saturated to create a focal tension against the warm surroundings.

title: Knell of the Mire
alt: A flooded ancient stone causeway in a misty swamp, a lone boatman visible in the distance

stages.label: Thumbnail
stages.text: The causeway perspective and the boatman's placement were tested across several thumbnails — the figure needed to read immediately as a narrative anchor but not dominate the environment.

stages.label: Final
stages.text: Layered atmospheric haze in cool grey-greens. The causeway's submerged stones were lit with a diffuse ambient bounce from the water — implying depth without high contrast.

title: The Stonespire Conclave
alt: An ancient gnarled tree shrine glowing with green energy, stone steps leading up to its door

stages.label: Thumbnail
stages.text: Explored whether the shrine should feel welcoming or foreboding. The upward-leading stone steps with a warm inner glow pushed the mood toward sacred rather than dangerous.

stages.label: Final
stages.text: Deep forest greens with a pulsing interior light. The gnarled tree was designed to appear as both architectural element and living entity — the boundary between natural and constructed deliberately blurred.
